meta.setting: rain-soaked wagon road below French Gulch
meta.rdr_clone_target: PS3-era RDR-clone western art, grounded in muddy travel and freight pressure

research_grounding.title: NPS Whiskeytown History and Culture
research_grounding.use: Road travel and extractive work in rugged Whiskeytown country.

research_grounding.title: California State Parks Shasta State Historic Park

image_prompt.subject: a freight wagon sunk to the hubs in a mud road, mule team straining, riders arguing, broken axle timber nearby, and boot tracks filling with rainwater
image_prompt.scene: cold rain break under gray sky; wet canvas sags over crates, a road crew watches with pry poles, no readable signs or labels
image_prompt.composition: square road-hazard scene with deep wheel ruts leading from foreground to the stuck wagon and hills beyond
image_prompt.camera: low road-level view close to the ruts, like a player approaching a blocked route
image_prompt.hand_cut_note: Make the mud look heavy enough to change the whole day.

style_modifiers.medium: PS3-era open-world western game art, early-HD in-engine road obstacle scene
render_traits: slightly simplified wagon team, riders, road crew, and muddy terrain
modest texture resolution on mud, wet wood, leather, canvas, cloth, and rainwater
hard ambient occlusion under wagon wheels, mule legs, crates, ruts, and people
flat gray post-rain light with wet highlights in the track
believable 1899 freight gear, clothing, and road tools
playable blocked-route composition with clear tracks and choices around the obstacle
style_modifiers.palette: mud umber, rain gray, wet wood brown, mule black, canvas tan, leather dark red-brown, pine green, and cold sky blue.

text_rendering.instructions: No readable crate labels, road signs, wagon names, paper, or text.

negative_prompt: copy of commercial game characters, missions, towns, or logos
empty muddy road with no travel problem

self_check: Reads as PS3-era western game art at square page-card scale.
The mud road, stuck freight wagon, mule team, broken axle, riders, and water-filled tracks are readable.
Every object belongs plausibly to rugged 1899 freight travel.
The image supports road delay, tracking, ambush timing, bargaining, and detour scenes.
No copied game characters, readable modern text, or inherited non-western symbols.
